threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep looking for a parking space like it’s a normal day, not a Saturday in Dalston when every car’s a battle.
Caught a lad on the 55 last night singing to his reflection in the window. Whole bus just watched—some laughed, others pretended to be asleep. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the guy at the corner bakery arguing with the baker over a forgotten croissant, it hit me—this is home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find spontaneous 'silent discos' in the parks, where folks dance with headphones on, completely lost in their own vibes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can decode the BART delays like it’s second nature, while the tourists are just looking around confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy trying to sell a hot dog during a Muni delay at Powell, claiming it was '95% more authentic than the ones at Fisherman’s Wharf.' Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think locals hang out in the squares all day, but we actually avoid them during siesta—too many loud visitors in the afternoons.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they stand in the middle of the street, phone in hand, staring blankly at a map instead of just looking up.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'corner shop' instead of the 'local' and find yourself confused by the endless vegan options.
Caught a glimpse of a guy holding a full fried chicken box on the 59 last night. He was negotiating the perfect dip into BBQ sauce while the bus swayed. Pure Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids setting up a makeshift café in the park, complete with hand-drawn menus. That’s the spirit of Neukölln!

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that turns into a bar after 10 PM, serving cocktails with döner meat on the side. It's wild.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they have a favorite taco spot that isn’t on Yelp and can argue about the best burrito on 24th.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus that stopped for five minutes because a guy was playing sax on the sidewalk. Classic SF vibe—music over schedule.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's squares are just for eating and drinking, but locals know they're actually for catching up with friends and spontaneous music jams.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by their reluctance to try the 'croqueta de jamón' from a local bar—they still think only tapas count.
